So when your sales are prospecting, which is phase number four of the DAP, you got to keep in mind one specific phrase called a CPR. CPR stands for a clear process to revenue. In business, when you are going about pinching people and talking to people about your ideas, your campaigns you want to run, all these goals and objectives that they have, you want to keep one phrase in mind. The CPR, the clear process to revenue. What are you doing and what are all these activities doing that are step by step going to get this person to revenue? What are you going to do to drive them revenue? How are you thinking to drive them revenue? Because this is what a business owner cares about. When you're talking to them about all this fancy mumbo jumbo that 90% of them don't understand, and the 10% that do understand only 50% of what you say don't understand the other 50%, you have to know that your CPR is what's going to get you to the next level. A CPR once again is the clear process to revenue. Let me give you some reality with what that means. Some actionable data around what I'm talking about right now, means that if you're in a pitch and you're telling somebody that you have all these Facebook ads you're going to do, and all these Google ads you're going to do, and you're going to retarget everybody, you've got to simplify it for the person you're pitching. Get it in a step by step way. Say something like this. So, client, we're going to take all these Facebook ads and Google ads and all that stuff after we create it all, and we're going to launch it. After we launch it, we should get leads to convert here. After they convert here, we should either A, get them to convert on a dollar value here, where we are now worth 10 bucks to us, or an email follow up here drives them back here, where we try to once again get them for 10 bucks. After we get them here, or after we skip over that step, we are then trying to convert them on that $99 sale that we have. Once again, another point of revenue here. So, the step by step process once again, and then repeat it to them how simple it is to get to the point of revenue. You can know you have a million, a million things you can tell them and talk to them about, that overwhelm them, don't talk nerd to them. Simplify it, be cool, calm down, CPR it. Step by step, what are you going to do to get them to a point of revenue? The same thing goes for your own ideas and your own ad campaigns in order to generate sales and prospects. What are you going to do for yourself in order to have a CPR? What is your step by step process of walking somebody through a sale to the point where you generate revenue? CPRs are really simple because that's what they're meant to do. Simplify the environment. They're meant to take all that fancy mumbo jumbo that you understand in this world over here, and it's meant to bridge a simplified version into their world over here of understanding and comprehension. All you need to do is lay out a CPR in your business for yourself, for your clients, and for anybody you sell. Watch your sales go through the roof and watch your ability to have people come out of confusion and come into an understanding and clarity inside of your business as soon as you mention this phrase to them. CPR. Clear process to revenue. I'll see you in the next video.
